title: The Belgian Gate Gap — Reed's Approach Observation
description: On the run-in, Reed observed a gap in the Belgian Gate obstacle line thirty yards west of the main approach — a slightly deeper water channel that a man could use to reach within thirty yards of the wire without crossing the MG-42's primary arc of fire. He did not report this because no one asked him. He is a coxswain, not an infantryman, and the information sat in him like ballast. If asked directly about the approach, or if shown the dead zone intelligence, he volunteers this immediately.
